q: In which year did World War II begin?
exp: It began with Germany's invasion of Poland in 1939 and ended in 1945.

q: Who led India's independence through non-violence?
exp: India became independent from Britain in 1947.

q: Which wall divided the city of Berlin until 1989?
exp: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, a symbol of the end of the Cold War.

q: Which was the first civilization to use writing?
exp: The Sumerians of Mesopotamia created cuneiform writing ~5,000 years ago.

q: Which Egyptian queen allied with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony?
exp: Cleopatra was the last pharaoh of Egypt, in the 1st century BC.

q: Which 1215 English document limited the king's power?
exp: Magna Carta is seen as a foundation of rights and constitutional rule.

q: Which 1789 revolution began with the storming of the Bastille?
Who was the first President of the United States?
exp: Washington took office in 1789, after independence in 1776.

q: Which Portuguese navigator reached India by sea in 1498?
exp: Vasco da Gama opened the sea route to India in 1498.

q: Which empire fell when Constantinople was captured in 1453?
exp: The Ottomans captured Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

q: Which 1492 treaty-era voyage was funded by Spain?
exp: Columbus sailed west for Spain hoping to reach Asia.

q: Who led the Soviet Union during most of World War II?
exp: Joseph Stalin led the USSR from 1924 until 1953.
